chunk_summary: This section outlines tele-nutrition protocols for monitoring growth in preterm and young infants under six months, emphasizing corrected age, growth charts, feeding safety, and supplementation guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of corrected age and appropriate growth charts for preterm infants.
Identify key tele-nutrition strategies for managing growth faltering in infants under six months.
Recognize feeding risks and supplementation needs in preterm and low birth weight infants.
key_facts: Preterm growth curves, such as Fenton charts, are used up to 50 weeks post-menstrual age, transitioning to WHO standards thereafter.
Weight loss >10% of birth weight should trigger feeding assessment and escalation if necessary.
Vitamin D prophylaxis is commonly 400 IU/day in infancy.
Preterm infants often require 2–3 mg/kg/day of enteral iron supplementation.
Reliable weight measurement should be obtained within 72 hours; otherwise, in-person measurement is recommended.
reference_quote: Fenton-type tools are used up to about 50 weeks post-menstrual age, then transitioned to term standards such as WHO charts.
